why: चयापचय and வளர்சிதை மாற்றம் are the established native biology terms taught in schools and natural in wellness content; a phonetic transliteration (मेटाबॉलिज़म) sounds foreign.
Everyday gym loanword used as-is by speakers; the native heart-exercise phrase reads textbook-formal and the literal heart translation is wrong for the exercise sense.
Tamil நீரேற்றம் is natural; in Hindi the idea is spoken as शरीर में पानी की मात्रा with जलयोजन as the single-word equivalent. A bare transliteration हाइड्रेशन is unnatural outside lab labels.
Hindi uses प्रोटीन as a fully naturalized everyday loanword; Tamil has the genuine native புரதம், both natural in diet talk.
Standard naturalized loanword in both languages; no native word competes in everyday diet and fitness speech.
पोषक तत्व and ஊட்டச்சத்து are the natural everyday words in food and diet content; a transliteration like न्यूट्रिएंट्स sounds clinical.
Strong native phrases used constantly in Indian wellness content; the transliteration इम्यूनिटी reads as marketing-English rather than proper localization.
पाचन and செரிமானம் are everyday native words for digestion, natural in food and gut-health talk.
सूजन and வீக்கம் are the natural spoken words for swelling/inflammation; widely understood colloquially.
मुद्रा (body posture, also yoga) and உடல் நிலை are natural native terms; the transliteration पोस्चर is reel-speak, not proper localization.
लचीलापन and நெகிழ்வுத்தன்மை are genuine natural words for bodily flexibility in fitness content.
सहनशक्ति and சகிப்புத்தன்மை (also தாங்கும் திறன்) are the natural native words for stamina/endurance.
Tamil மீட்பு / மீட்சி is a genuine native term; in Hindi the idea is spoken as शरीर का ठीक होना / आराम, with रिकवरी also common in gym register. The native sense is clearly expressible.
Tamil நீட்சி / நீட்டிப்பு பயிற்சி is a real native phrase and खिंचाव conveys the stretch naturally; स्ट्रेचिंग loanword is also common in Hindi gyms.
Naturalized gym loanword in both languages; the literal heat-up phrase (शरीर गरम करना) sounds odd as a fitness instruction.
Fixed gym jargon; the literal repetition translation (पुनरावृत्ति / மறுபடி எண்ணிக்கை) is never said while training and would confuse.
Fixed gym jargon ('three sets of ten') used as-is; a literal group translation (समूह / தொகுப்பு) would be absurd in a workout instruction.
Universal everyday fitness loanword; native व्यायाम/கசரत and உடற்பயிற்சி exist but workout is what gym culture actually says.
व्यायाम (also कसरत) and உடற்பயிற்சி are strong, natural native words used everywhere; transliteration एक्सरसाइज़ is colloquial but not proper localization.
Equipment name kept as-is; there is no practical native word and a coined translation would be unrecognizable in a gym.
Product/equipment name used as-is universally; native paraphrases are never spoken and any literal coinage would be absurd.
Clinical loanword spoken in English by doctors and patients; no native equivalent is in everyday use.
Commonly kept as ब्लड प्रेशर/BP in speech; native रक्तचाप / ரத்த அழுத்தம் exist but the English/BP form dominates clinical and casual conversation.
Spoken as डायबिटीज/शुगर in Hindi and சர்க்கரை நோய்/diabetes in Tamil; the English/sugar form is the everyday clinical usage even though मधुमेह / நீரிழிவு exist.
कमी (e.g. विटामिन की कमी) and குறைபாடு are the natural words; a transliteration डेफिशियेंसी is awkward.
Product/nutrition-store term used as-is; literal additive translations (पूरक / நிரப்பி) sound clinical and aren't used for protein or vitamin supplements.
Technical product/marketing loanword spoken in English; native coinages exist in textbooks but are never used in everyday wellness speech.
Everyday wellness/marketing loanword; the literal toxin-removal phrase (विषहरण / நச்சு நீக்கம்) sounds clinical and is not how people refer to detox routines.
थकान and சோர்வு / களைப்பு are everyday native words for tiredness/fatigue in health and fitness talk.
जोड़ and மூட்டு are the natural anatomy words used in pain and mobility content; a transliteration जॉइंट्स is not proper localization.
मांसपेशी and தசை are strong, natural native words; Hindi gym register borrows मसल but मांसपेशी is the correct localization.
Universal everyday loanword for vitamins; no native word competes in common speech.
Fixed fitness slang for abdominal definition; a literal six-package translation would be absurd, so the English term is kept.
Fixed supplement-product term used as-is in gyms; a literal before-exercise translation would not be recognized as the product.
दमखम / सहनशक्ति and சகிப்புத்தன்மை are natural native words for stamina; the स्टैमिना loanword is also common but the native forms read naturally.
मोटापा and உடல் பருமன் are the everyday native words for obesity/being overweight, natural in health talk.
वज़न घटाना and எடை குறைப்பு are completely natural native phrases used universally in diet and fitness conversation.
